summary: Soundboard capture of a stellar 1970 benefit show with an epic Lovelight featuring Peter Green and a raging Other One, good overall sound despite early mix hiccups.
review: The performance is praised for its extended Pigpen‑driven Lovelight (with guest guitarist Peter Green) and a monster The Other One that many reviewers call a rager.  While the first couple of tunes suffer from a slightly off‑balance mix and an overly forward Bob vocal, the rest of the set sounds clear and balanced, delivering strong banter and a Furthur‑era vibe.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 5 stars) highlight standout songs and overall enjoyment; only minor recording mix issues are noted, leading to a strong combined show‑and‑recording rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix is crisp for most of the set but the first couple of tunes are slightly off‑balance with Bob up in the mix; overall clarity and balance are solid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show stretches out with a Furthur‑era feel, featuring lively stage banter and teased tunes; the extended Lovelight adds momentum after earlier acoustic material.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Trading licks with Peter Green during Lovelight is highlighted as a favorite moment.
band_member_comments.Phil: Brief Dark Star riff mentioned, but no detailed commentary on his bass playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Early mix places Bob (referred to as Bobby) prominently in the front of the soundstage.
